steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between two independent clauses joined by the conjunctive adverb however: He did not play drums on the label's classic song "Rapper's [blank] and he joined the label after the song's release. The correct punctuation must separate two independent clauses and handle the quotation mark and however correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Delight." However,': a period ends the first sentence correctly, but "However," then starts the second clause as a fragment-opener followed only by a comma — a conjunctive adverb between two independent clauses still needs a semicolon after it, not a comma. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Delight," however;': the comma before the closing quotation mark is standard; the semicolon after however properly links the two independent clauses around the conjunctive adverb. This is the correct pattern: [IC], conjunctive adverb; [IC]. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Delight," however,': uses a comma both before and after however, creating a comma splice — two independent clauses joined by only commas. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Delight" however': no punctuation around however at all. The two independent clauses run together with no separator. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
